👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:

videos.title: STACKING MOMENTUM! How the Carolina Hurricanes can Go Another Level In Round 2
videos.summary_source_text:
STACKING MOMENTUM! How the Carolina Hurricanes can Go Another Level In Round 2 While the Carolina Hurricanes still wait for their Round 1 opponent, let's discuss what hopefully happens in Round 2, regardless of who they face. On the Wednesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach starts the show by sharing his thoughts on how the Hurricanes' power play needs to refind it's form after finishing 4th in the league during the regular season. As of now, it's tied for 11th at 13.3% with the Philadelphia Flyers. Regarding the penalty kill, it's humming along as the second-best in the playoffs. Can the man-advantage get to that level next round? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on how he's looking at the top line to make its mark on the postseason, after combining for five points in Round 1. While they have a proven track record of being clutch, the hope is that it breaks out big time to lead the way. Finally, Zach concludes the show by giving his thoughts on who he is looking at early in terms of who to watch for Round 2. While he will give his overall analysis for the Round 2 crossover, it's never too early to scout ahead. All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: What Happens If You Stop Eating Sugar for 14 Days – Dr. Berg On Quitting Sugar Cravings
videos.summary_source_text:
What Happens If You Stop Eating Sugar for 14 Days – Dr. Berg On Quitting Sugar Cravings Some amazing things can happen if you stop eating sugar. Check this out. 

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

👉 Get Dr. Berg’s Daily Routine as a free download: https://drbrg.co/3QuuXiY
 
Timestamps
0:10 What would happen if you stopped eating sugar
3:38 Fat-burning
4:05 More benefits of quitting sugar

In this video, we’re going to talk about what happens to your body if you stop eating sugar for 14 days. What you’re doing is converting from sugar burning to fat burning. This can take about three days, and you may feel a few symptoms in the process. Taking B vitamins and potassium can help with these symptoms. 
 
What would happen if you stopped eating sugar: 

1. Lose appetite for sugar – 
Every time you consume sugar, a hormone comes in there and pushes the sugars down, causing a low blood sugar situation that causes you to crave sugar. 

2. Less hungry – 
It’s the sugar that keeps you hungry all the time. Giving up sugar can help stabilize blood sugars.

3. Less fatigue – 
Especially after eating a meal. 

4. Lose excess water and fat – 
For the first week, you’re going to dump a lot of excess water and more fat.

5. Enhanced mood and improved cognitive function — 
More focus and concentration.

6. Skin looks better — 
Glowing skin with less acne. 

7. Less stiffness – 
Less inflammation and less pain.

More benefits of quitting sugar:
• Build new enzymes to run the body on fat fuel 
• Less inflammation in the arteries 
• Growing brain cells 
• Clean out a fatty liver 
• Better kidney function 

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

Dr. Berg is a licensed chiropractor in Virginia, California, and Louisiana, but he no longer practices chiropractic in any state and does not see patients, so he can focus on educating people as a full-time activity, yet he maintains an active license. This video is for general informational purposes only. It should not be used to self-diagnose, and it is not a substitute for a medical exam, cure, treatment, diagnosis, prescription, or recommendation. videos.title: UK | 28th April, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 28th April, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation 28/04/26

🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 28/04/26

videos.title: Square the Club Face for Better Lag #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Square the Club Face for Better Lag #golfswing #golftips Many golfers struggle to hold lag and think it’s just a wrist or arm angle issue. The truth is that an open club face ruins lag before it even starts.
To retain lag effectively, square the club face, allow proper forearm rotation, and flex the wrist naturally while controlling the pivot. Proper sequencing of the body will naturally create lag — but you’ll only hit good shots if the face is presented correctly at impact.
Follow for more precise, practical coaching from Me and My Golf. Many golfers struggle to hold lag and think it’s just a wrist or arm angle issue. The truth is that an open club face ruins lag before it even starts.
To retain lag effectively, square the club face, allow proper forearm rotation, and flex the wrist naturally while controlling the pivot. Proper sequencing of the body will naturally create lag — but you’ll only hit good shots if the face is presented correctly at impact.
Follow for more precise, practical coaching from Me and My Golf.

videos.title: Nathan MacKinnon NOT a Finalist For the Ted Lindsay. Are His Fellow NHLers Bored With Him?
videos.summary_source_text:
Nathan MacKinnon NOT a Finalist For the Ted Lindsay. Are His Fellow NHLers Bored With Him? Colorado Avalanche fans are stunned as Nathan MacKinnon, who led the NHL in goals and even-strength points—matching Wayne Gretzky-era levels—gets snubbed as a Ted Lindsay Award finalist. Can MacKinnon turn this slight into fuel for another dominant playoff run?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson debate the surprise omission, break down the award voting trends, and compare MacKinnon’s season to stars like Connor McDavid, Nikita Kucherov, and Macklin Celebrini. The conversation shifts to Avalanche Round 1 player stats, spotlighting standout performances by Artturi Lehkonen and Cale Makar, while examining who needs to step up offensively in the next round. Plus, an NHL overtime goal controversy sparks talk of much-needed rule changes. Will the Avalanche harness this chip-on-the-shoulder energy as they await their next playoff opponent?

videos.title: The Crosby Dangle Pattern
videos.summary_source_text:
The Crosby Dangle Pattern The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: Don’t Contract, Relax!
videos.summary_source_text:
Don’t Contract, Relax! Power skating tells you to sit low in a chair, tense your muscles and push as hard as possible to stride.

Inside The Downhill Skating System movements like the Anchor mechanic proves the opposite.

Relax the off leg and let your inside edge do the work, and you’ll be moving more efficient and skating with less effort.

This is just one piece of the Downhill Skating System where we teach that exact fundamentals with lessons, drills and video to skate more like McDavid, Barzal and MacKinnon, the games best skaters.

Inside Train 2.0+, you’ll get the full system, plus 10+ courses on the mechanics behind today’s best players.

1/2 cup (100 g) walnuts. 1/3 cup (60 g) pumpkin seeds.
Like this video to save this healthy bread recipe and keep it from getting lost.
1 cup (120 g) rolled oats.
1/3 cup (60 g) dried cranberries.
2 eggs.
1 cup (240 ml) warm water.
Salt.
1/4 cup (60 g) psyllium husk.
Let the dough rest for 1 hour.
Olive oil.
Sesame seeds.
Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for about 60 minutes.
If you enjoy sugar-free recipes, subscribe to Schnelle Rezepte – it's all about healthy eating!

Still using ChatGPT or stitching together 4 different tools for your systematic literature review? There's a better way.

In this video, I walk you through SciSpace's new SLR Agent — a fully customisable, PRISMA-compliant systematic literature review tool that screened 3,000+ papers in under 60 minutes. I tested it head-to-head against ChatGPT and dedicated review tools like Rayyan, Covidence, and Elicit, and the results genuinely surprised me.

I also break down SciSpace's new Skills feature — including Clinical Trial Search, Grant Research, Web Search, and Presentation Generation — that turn this from a literature review tool into a full research operating system.

videos.title: Get More Speed | Part 2 | Padraig Harrington
videos.summary_source_text:
Get More Speed | Part 2 | Padraig Harrington As part of my YouTube series  “Ten lessons every golfer should know”, the third Paddy’s Golf Tip covers “Speed”, which is a two-part video. 
 
In this second instalment, I explain why a short swing and stopping at impact in practise and playing with a normal length swing on the course works for me and also why releasing the arms, hands and the club is vital in order to maximise swingspeed.  I demonstrate other techniques including moving the feet at address and explain my use of carbon fibre shoe insoles and a training aid.

#PadraigHarrington #golfing #golftips #paddysgolftips #golf #feedback #vktrygolf #superspeedgolf

SUBSCRIBE FOR MORE CONTENT: https://bit.ly/4gKoVme

videos.title: 464. Finansiell rådgivning för privatpersoner med Ricard Nylund
videos.summary_source_text:
464. Finansiell rådgivning för privatpersoner med Ricard Nylund Vad är en finansiell rådgivare egentligen värd – och behöver du en?

I det här avsnittet har vi tillbaka Ricard Nylund från Ricap, mannen bakom
det numera bevingade uttrycket "Jan, när ska du släppa taget om
handbromsen?". Vi reder ut de vanligaste misstagen sparare gör på alla
förmögenhetsnivåer, och varför värdet av rådgivning sällan ligger där du
tror.

🎯 DET VANLIGASTE MISSTAGET
De tre fallgroparna Ricard ser om och om igen är överaktivitet, för låg
risk och för hög risk. Överaktivitet är det dyraste — du är med på
nedgångarna men missar uppgångarna. Och två personer med samma globala
indexfond kan ligga i helt olika risknivåer beroende på resten av deras
ekonomi.

🏗️ RICARDS PROCESS – NERIFRÅN OCH UPP
Likvidbuffert för det oväntade. Reservbuffert i lågriskplaceringar så att
livet inte sätts på paus när börsen dyker. Och sist potentialen — motorn
som ska få ligga ifred och växa. Ordningen är inte förhandlingsbar.

🏠 DEN RIKE FATTIGPENSIONÄREN
Kanske avsnittets mest konträra poäng: skuldfrihet är inte samma sak som
ekonomisk frihet. Vi pratar om varför "mest belånad vinner" — givet att
grunden är stabil — och varför ett amorteringsrace ibland kostar dig den
frihet du faktiskt sparat ihop till.

👥 FYRA TYPFALL
Anna och Martin (45, barnfamilj). Emma (30, singel, 50 000 kr sparat).
Sara (samma utgångspunkt men driver eget). Joakim (10 miljoner i
investerbart kapital). Plus den provocerande observationen att de sämsta
portföljerna Ricard ser ofta finns hos personer med 30–80 miljoner —
inte hos nybörjarna.

❓ ERA FRÅGOR
I del två svarar vi på det ni skickat in: amortera eller investera, sälja
aktier som gått dåligt, skatteplanera som en miljardär, vad göra med
plötsliga arvspengar, hitta en bra rådgivare, och vad FIRE-rörelsen ofta
missar.

✨ EN GENOMGÅENDE TRÅD
Ricard är förmodligen Sveriges tråkigaste finansiella rådgivare — och det
säger han själv. Hans mål är inte att slå index. Hans mål är att du ska
må bra av dina pengar. Och det är det här avsnittet handlar om.

Många hälsningar,
Jan, Caroline och Ricard

Finansiell rådgivning med Ricard
====
Eftersom Ricard driver eget har han inte obegränsat med tid och möjligheter. Därav att jag skriver detta för att vara schysst både mot honom och dig. 

Eftersom varje rådgivning enligt lag kräver en mycket arbete, främst i framtagande av rådgivningsdokumentation, blir den procentuella kostnaden oskäligt hög om du inte har ett investerbart kapital på över 500.000 kr exkl. pension, alternativt har icke-kollektivavtalsanslutna tjänstepensionstillgångar runt 2.000.000 kr eller driver ett AB.

Enklast är att boka tid med Ricard via denna Calendly-länk: https://calendly.com/ricap/radgivning-rikatillsammans
Hans mejl och mobiltelefon hittar du på hans hemsida: https://ricap.se/kontakt/

Länkar
=====
Så hittar du en bra finansiell rådgivare – tips, frågor och forskning: https://rikatillsammans.se/spara-och-investera/ekonomisk-radgivning-och-finansiella-radgivare/
Diskussion till avsnittet i forumet: https://rikatillsammans.se/forum/t/120766

Innehållsförteckning
======
00:00:00 - Intro till avsnittet
00:03:39 - Tre vanligaste misstagen: överaktivitet, för lite risk och för mycket risk
00:08:09 - De flesta saknar en plan för vad pengarna ska användas till
00:11:00 - En finansiell rådgivare trollar inte fram bättre avkastning
00:12:39 - Resonemang om belåning när grunden är stabil
00:14:09 - Planera för ett långt liv, inte ett slutdatum 
00:15:00 - Den bästa finansiella planen är den du faktiskt kan hålla
00:18:45 - Drömmar skjuts ofta upp för länge och hinner aldrig bli verklighet
00:19:27 - FIRE - hiss eller diss?
00:22:45 - Ricards syn på buffert
00:28:33 - Scenario: Barnfamiljen runt 45 år med lön, barn hemma och belånat boende
00:32:18 - Scenario: Ung singel på 30 år med 50 000 kr sparade – vad är viktigast nu?
00:34:00 - Scenario: Företagaren i uppstartsfas
00:37:42 - Scenario: Joakim med 10 miljoner i investerbart kapital
00:39:57 - Hur pengarna har tjänats ihop påverkar hur man förvaltar dem 
00:46:18 - Scenario: Entreprenören som gjort exit
00:49:57 - Varför anlita en finansiell rådgivare om man ändå ska ha en global indexfond?
00:50:57 - Sätt upp en plan, måla upp livet och låt pengarna vara
00:54:18 - Vilket ekonomiskt beslut idag ger störst effekt om tio år?
01:01:12 - Tjänstepension vs. kapitalförsäkring i eget bolag
01:01:39 - Hur hittar man en bra finansiell rådgivare? 
01:07:27 - Ärver snart en större summa – tips?
01:09:00 - Hur stor förmögenhet bör man ha innan man köper lyxvaror som en sportbil?
01:11:06 - Hur räknar man ut rätt storlek på buffert och reservbuffert?
01:12:12 - Ska man berätta för sin sambo hur mycket man fått i förskottsarv?
01:15:00 - Guld, råvaror och allvädersportföljer – hiss eller diss?

====
Eftersom Ricard driver eget har han inte obegränsat med tid och möjligheter. Därav att jag skriver detta för att vara schysst både mot honom och dig. 

Eftersom varje rådgivning enligt lag kräver en mycket arbete, främst i framtagande av rådgivningsdokumentation, blir den procentuella kostnaden oskäligt hög om du inte har ett investerbart kapital på över 500.000 kr exkl. pension, alternativt har icke-kollektivavtalsanslutna tjänstepensionstillgångar runt 2.000.000 kr eller driver ett AB.

Enklast är att boka tid med Ricard via denna Calendly-länk: https://calendly.com/ricap/radgivning-rikatillsammans
Hans mejl och mobiltelefon hittar du på hans hemsida: https://ricap.se/kontakt/

Länkar
=====
Så hittar du en bra finansiell rådgivare – tips, frågor och forskning: https://rikatillsammans.se/spara-och-investera/ekonomisk-radgivning-och-finansiella-radgivare/
Diskussion till avsnittet i forumet: https://rikatillsammans.se/forum/t/120766

Innehållsförteckning
======

00:03:39 - Tre vanligaste misstagen: överaktivitet, för lite risk och för mycket risk
00:08:09 - De flesta saknar en plan för vad pengarna ska användas till
00:11:00 - En finansiell rådgivare trollar inte fram bättre avkastning
00:12:39 - Resonemang om belåning när grunden är stabil
00:14:09 - Planera för ett långt liv, inte ett slutdatum 
00:15:00 - Den bästa finansiella planen är den du faktiskt kan hålla
00:18:45 - Drömmar skjuts ofta upp för länge och hinner aldrig bli verklighet
00:19:27 - FIRE - hiss eller diss?
00:22:45 - Ricards syn på buffert
00:28:33 - Scenario: Barnfamiljen runt 45 år med lön, barn hemma och belånat boende
00:32:18 - Scenario: Ung singel på 30 år med 50 000 kr sparade – vad är viktigast nu?
00:34:00 - Scenario: Företagaren i uppstartsfas

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

The most dangerous fat you can put in your body isn’t butter, egg yolks, tallow, or even coconut oil. It’s something far more common: industrial seed oils like corn oil, soybean oil, canola oil, and cottonseed oil.

These oils are found in almost every restaurant meal, salad dressing, and processed food. They’re marketed as “heart-healthy,” but the truth is very different.

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

videos.title: SHINE ON! Carolina Hurricanes' 3 Stars of Round 1 Against the Ottawa Senators
videos.summary_source_text:
SHINE ON! Carolina Hurricanes' 3 Stars of Round 1 Against the Ottawa Senators While the Carolina Hurricanes wait on who they'll face in Round 2, let's look back on Round 1. When taking a look again at Round 1, who are the three stars against the Ottawa Senators?  Was it a whole line? Was it goaltending? Depth maybe? On the Tuesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach starts the show by sharing his thoughts on his combined 3rd Star of Logan Stankoven and Jackson Blake. The two young players played well throughout the series. Could one of them be moved higher? Could someone else have made the three-star list? Why does it make sense to combine them? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on why Frederik Andersen is the 2nd Star of the series. Is there an argument to move Andersen to the 1st Star after his phenomenal Round 1? Will he keep this pace going? Finally, Zach concludes the show by sharing why Taylor Hall just edges out Andersen for the 1st Star. videos.title: This BMW has a BIG PROBLEM
videos.summary_source_text:
This BMW has a BIG PROBLEM Explore new and used cars with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-2804

It’s time for three of the most sought-after electric SUVs to go head-to-head to see which deserves a spot on your driveway. 

In one corner, we have the global best-seller - and recently updated - Tesla Model Y. It is being challenged by a fresh, high-tech rival from Ingolstadt, the Audi Q6 Sportback e-tron, and the latest all-electric offering from Munich, the BMW iX3. 

These three represent the cutting edge of premium family transport, but they each take a very different approach to design, performance, and daily usability.

The Tesla Model Y AWD Long Range remains the benchmark that everyone else is trying to beat. Known for its class-leading efficiency and unparalleled charging infrastructure, the Model Y offers a dual-motor setup that provides sure-footed all-wheel-drive traction and a 0-60mph time that puts many sports cars to shame. 

Inside, it’s as minimalist as every car in Tesla’s line-up, dominated by a single massive touchscreen that controls almost every function of the car. While some may find the lack of physical buttons polarising, there is no denying the sheer amount of interior space and the clever storage solutions, including a massive "frunk" and a cavernous rear boot.

Moving to the Audi Q6 Sportback RWD Performance, we see a focus on sleek aesthetics and long-distance touring. By opting for the rear-wheel-drive "Performance" variant, Audi has prioritised range, utilising a large battery and a more aerodynamic Sportback silhouette to extract maximum miles from every charge. 

Built on the new PPE platform shared with Porsche, the Q6 features an ultra-fast 800V charging architecture, allowing you to add hundreds of miles in the time it takes to grab a coffee. The interior features Audi’s new "Digital Stage" OLED display layout, although we’re not a fan of the huge bezels around the screen! Thankfully, though, there are high-quality materials throughout. 

The BMW iX3 50 xDrive represents the latest evolution of BMW's electric SUV lineup, now boasting a dual-motor configuration for significantly more punch than its predecessor. BMW has focused heavily on the driving experience, ensuring that even as a heavy SUV, it retains that signature "Ultimate Driving Machine" DNA with sharp steering and composed handling. 

It also features a massive battery capacity that pushes its range toward the 500-mile mark in ideal conditions, and like the Audi, it utilises an 800V system for lightning-fast top-ups. The cabin features the all-new panoramic iVision display, which, while impressive, potentially offers a lot of information that isn’t that useful on a trip to the shops! 

This group test isn't just about the numbers on a spec sheet; it’s about how these cars handle the real world. Mat will be putting all three through their paces, testing everything from their real-world efficiency and interior quality to their infotainment systems and rear-seat practicality. By the end of this deep dive, we’ll see if the Tesla can hold onto its crown or if the German giants have finally caught up to - and overtaken - the most popular electric SUV of recent years!

Chapters

00:12 Motor & 0-60 Performance Specs
00:34 EV Drag Race: Audi vs Tesla vs BMW

16:17 Front Boot (Frunk) Comparison
17:31 UK Price & Value Analysis
18:03 Verdict: Which Electric SUV is Best? Explore new and used cars with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-2804

videos.title: Intermittent Fasting Doubles Your Risk of Dying from a Heart Attack
videos.summary_source_text:
Intermittent Fasting Doubles Your Risk of Dying from a Heart Attack Recent news reports have claimed that intermittent fasting can lead to cardiovascular death. What’s the truth?

DATA:
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10115489/
https://link.springer.com/article/10.1186/s12967-020-02687-0
https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnut.2021.669325/full 
https://www.jacc.org/doi/full/10.1016/j.jaccao.2022.03.002

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

👉 Get Dr. Berg’s Daily Routine as a free download: https://drbrg.co/4vKsp01

0:00 Introduction: Does fasting cause cardiovascular problems?
1:09 Poor data on intermittent fasting
2:36 Peer-reviewed studies supporting intermittent fasting 
4:26 Fasting does not increase cardiovascular risk

Today, we’re going to talk about some of the recent news headlines claiming that fasting can wreak havoc on your cardiovascular system. This information was taken from an abstract (short summary) of an observational study sponsored by the American Heart Association.

The study was based on a 24-hour dietary recall, where participants had to recall everything they ate over a period of time. The author has also conducted observational studies that directly opposed red meat, eggs, and cholesterol. 

Credible, peer-reviewed studies have shown numerous benefits of intermittent fasting and show that intermittent fasting actually supports cardiovascular health. 

1. A single-blind randomized study examined the long-term effects of time-restricted eating with resistance training over 12 months. Participants saw a significant decrease in body mass, fat mass, and inflammation.

They experienced increased insulin sensitivity and a more balanced lipid profile without adverse effects. Intermittent fasting was deemed safe and effective for improving cardiovascular function. 

2. A non-randomized controlled clinical trial for time-restricted feeding and its effect on body composition and cardiovascular risk found that 18 hours of fasting significantly decreased waist circumference and cardiovascular risk. 

3. A systematic review of intermittent fasting with cardiometabolic risk factors looked at four randomized controlled trials. The results demonstrated that intermittent fasting significantly reduced body weight, waist circumference, body mass, fat mass, blood pressure, cholesterol, triglycerides, and fasting insulin levels.

4. One study evaluated a 16-hour fasting eating pattern with an 8-hour eating window on breast cancer survivors for 8 weeks. Results demonstrated a significant decrease in cardiovascular risk. 

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 59, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the author of the best-selling book "The Healthy Keto Plan" and is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

videos.title: UK | 27th April, Monday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 27th April, Monday - RIFT Presentation 27/04/26

🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 27/04/26

videos.title: Mathew Barzal Wide Forward Stride
videos.summary_source_text:
Mathew Barzal Wide Forward Stride The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: Connor McDavid’s Counterintuitive Attack Pattern
videos.summary_source_text:
Connor McDavid’s Counterintuitive Attack Pattern In this video, we’re diving into Connor McDavid’s drive wide guide. Whether you’re a fan of hockey superstar Connor McDavid or simply aiming to up your game, we’ve got an exciting formula to share with you.

You might be wondering, what’s the secret behind McDavid’s net driving success? Hint: it’s not all about his speed. There’s a strategic approach that sets him apart. And the best part? You don’t need to be the fastest skater out there to master this technique. 

Let’s break it down step by step. McDavid’s winning formula begins when he gains possession of the puck between the dots, positioning himself strategically within this zone. Step two involves a crucial move which is a crossover or weight shift towards the boards. This creates space between him and the defenseman, a key advantage that you’ll witness in action.

But here comes the game-changer: step three. McDavid initiates crossing over towards the defenseman. This isn’t just about speed; it’s about efficiency and deception. Crossing over proves to be not only faster than regular forward skating but also incredibly deceptive, giving him the edge he needs.

Contrast this with the typical approaches other players take. They might rush straight down from the boards or head directly into the corner, missing out on the strategy that makes McDavid’s drives so effective. His pattern starts between the dots, widens out, and he smartly crosses over towards the defenseman, giving him the upper hand every time. videos.title: Claude Mythos: The AI Anthropic Refuses to Release (And Why It Matters)
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Mythos: The AI Anthropic Refuses to Release (And Why It Matters) Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Anthropic just built Claude Mythos Preview — a frontier AI model that found thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities in seven weeks, including flaws in every major operating system and browser. Then they refused to release it.

In this video, I break down what Claude Mythos actually is, what it can really do (with the actual benchmark numbers), the messy Capybara leak that forced Anthropic's hand, and the four real reasons behind the lockdown — ranked by how likely each one is.

We cover Project Glasswing, the AISI evaluation, the unauthorized-access incident, and what this all means for developers, security pros, and anyone who builds software online. Plus the skeptical take: is this a genuine watershed moment, or is Sam Altman right that it's "fear-based marketing"?

videos.title: DeepSeek V4 Explained: 1.6 Trillion Parameters & 1 Million Token Context
videos.summary_source_text:
DeepSeek V4 Explained: 1.6 Trillion Parameters & 1 Million Token Context Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
DeepSeek V4 just dropped — 1.6 trillion parameters, 1 million token context, fully open source. In this video, I break down exactly what makes V4 different, how the architecture actually works, and whether it can really compete with GPT-5.4, Claude, and Gemini 3.

🔍 WHAT'S COVERED:
00:00 - Intro
01:31 - Who Is DeepSeek and Why Should You Care?
02:59 - The Key Features That Make V4 Different
05:17 - Technical Deep Dive
07:52 - V4 vs. The Frontier: GPT-4o, Llama 3, Claude 3, Gemini 3
10:20 - A Real Demo: What V4 Actually Does
11:48 - Practical Use Cases You Can Actually Run Today
13:26 - The Limitations You Need to Know About

⚡ KEY SPECS:
- V4-Pro: 1.6T total parameters, 49B active per token
- V4-Flash: 284B total, 13B active
- 1,000,000 token context window (default)
- MIT licensed — fully open weights
- V4-Flash: $0.14/M input, $0.28/M output

🧠 ARCHITECTURE HIGHLIGHTS:
✅ Mixture-of-Experts with 384 distributed experts
✅ Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention
✅ Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections
✅ Trained with the new Muon optimizer
✅ FP4 precision on expert weights

🆚 HOW IT COMPARES:
- Largest open-weight LLM ever released
- Matches GPT-5.4 on coding benchmarks
- ~4x cheaper than Claude 3.5 Haiku on output tokens
- Ties GPT-4o for longest context window
- Text-only (no vision or audio)

💼 BEST USE CASES:
- Long-document Q&A (legal, research, finance)
- Full codebase analysis & agentic coding
- High-volume conversational AI
- Multilingual applications
- Custom fine-tuned deployments


📌 RELATED VIDEOS:
- GPT-5.5 Full Review: https://youtu.be/5iQcpXSpv2c 
- Claude vs Chatgpt Comparison: https://youtu.be/Om4bU0iaLAc

💬 Question: Would you actually deploy V4 in production, or is the closed-model ecosystem still worth the premium? Let me know in the comments.

👍 If this helped, hit the like button — it genuinely makes a difference.
🔔 Subscribe for more honest AI deep-dives.

Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is here - Show up #1 in ChatGPT *Start a free trial of HubSpot's new AEO tool:* https://clickhubspot.com/8g0k

I walk through how I use HubSpot’s AEO tool to check brand visibility in AI search.
AI search is changing how people find brands. More people now ask ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity for advice, ideas, and product choices. That means brands need to know how they show up in AI answer engines.

I show how answer engine optimization, or AEO, works inside HubSpot. I go through the free trial setup, add a brand, pick competitors, choose an ideal customer, and review the prompts HubSpot tracks.

Then I look at the AEO dashboard. I check brand visibility, brand sentiment, share of voice, prompt results, citations, and content recommendations. This helps me see how my brand shows up in ChatGPT and other AI tools.
I also show how HubSpot gives clear next steps, like what content to make and which citations matter. This can help improve AI visibility, AEO results, and how often your brand shows up in AI answers.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

• Amazing golf courses

videos.title: I Tested Golf’s Biggest Myth…The Results Surprised Me!
videos.summary_source_text:
I Tested Golf’s Biggest Myth…The Results Surprised Me! In this episode, we finally put the ultimate debate to the test: Would you score lower if a Pro hit all your drives, or if a Pro hit all your approach shots? Most golfers are told that "it's all about the short game," but the data often tells a different story. We took 10-handicapper Rory Anderson out on the course to experiment with both scenarios. We analyse everything from penalty strokes off the tee to proximity to the hole on approach shots to see which professional intervention actually saves the most strokes for the average player.

What You’ll Learn:
The Impact of Distance: How a pro-level drive changes your club selection for the rest of the hole.

Penalty Avoidance: Why the driver might be the most "destructive" club for an amateur.

Proximity to Hole: Does a pro's iron play actually lead to more birdies for a 10 handicap?

Course Management: Why knowing your "miss" is the secret to breaking 80.

00:30 Hole 1 (Driving)

12:19 Hole 5 (Approach)

19:51 Hole 7 (Approach)
22:49 Final Hole (Approach)
27:56 My Verdict

videos.title: The BEST putter L.A.B have EVER made 👀
videos.summary_source_text:
The BEST putter L.A.B have EVER made 👀 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: Why You Are So TIRED (It's This Deficiency)
videos.summary_source_text:
Why You Are So TIRED (It's This Deficiency) There are many possible reasons why you feel tired all the time. It’s important to identify and address the root cause, not the symptom. Find out how to boost energy naturally by addressing the most common chronic fatigue causes at the source. 

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

0:00 Introduction: Why am I always tired? 
0:23 Constant fatigue causes 
1:22 Fatigue and blood sugar
4:26 Latent virus fatigue
6:56 Low oxygen and chronic fatigue
9:13 Low vitamin B1 and fatigue symptoms
13:30 Adrenal fatigue

Keep watching for more energy-boosting tips and more ways to improve your sleep:
▶️ https://youtu.be/I2GApkHYVIo
▶️ https://youtu.be/pJ9xTMkGxXg
▶️ https://youtu.be/gGuF6X1cd_Y

Mold video: 
https://youtu.be/ngE3q-fnz1I

Healthy Ketogenic Diet and IF: 
https://youtu.be/vMZfyEy_jpI

Download Dr. Berg's Free Daily Health Routine: https://drbrg.co/4txQLZm

Stimulating the body with caffeine can actually make fatigue worse. Instead of masking the problem, you need to get to the root cause of fatigue. Once you address it, you can often get rid of chronic fatigue completely.

Not getting enough sleep significantly affects cognitive function. It also impacts blood sugar, increasing cravings and leading to more snacking. If you have insomnia, focus on simple habits like taking long walks in nature during the day without your cell phone, stretching before bed, dimming the lights at night, and avoiding snacking before sleep.

If your blood sugar is constantly going up and down, you’re going to feel tired. Even if there’s plenty of food available, your cells aren’t getting the fuel they need. One of the key solutions is to eliminate sugar and excess carbs from the diet.

A slow thyroid, histamines, latent viruses, and electrolyte imbalances are some of the most common hidden causes of fatigue. Even a lack of sun exposure could be the reason behind chronic fatigue.

videos.summary_source_text:
READ IT & SWEEP! Carolina Hurricanes Swept the Ottawa Senators Following their Game 4 Win The Carolina Hurricanes swept the Ottawa Senators with a 4-2 win in Game 4 in Ottawa on Saturday night. What stood out in a chaotic game that saw the Hurricanes advance to Round 2? On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach starts the show by sharing his thoughts on Game 4 as a whole. From the goals, the saves, to the chaos, and so much more. It was a game of attrition, but in the end, the Hurricanes swept the Senators with their 4-2 win. Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on the series as a whole, especially with how the Hurricanes performed in all four games. What stood out the most, leading to the series sweep? Finally, Zach concludes the show by sharing his thoughts on who the Hurricanes could face in Round 1 between either the Pittsburgh Penguins or the Philadelphia Flyers. With them playing Game 5 on Monday night, who could take that game? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: Bernd Wiesberger's Winning Back 9 | Volvo China Open
videos.summary_source_text:
Bernd Wiesberger's Winning Back 9 | Volvo China Open Watch the drama unfold in the Back 9 on Sunday, presented by Nexo, as Bernd Wiesberger clinched victory at the Volvo China Open. He turned a three-shot deficit into a two-shot win to secure his ninth DP World Tour title. His previous win came 5 years ago in Farsø, Denmark where he beat the likes of Robert MacIntyre and Min Woo Lee.
